response:
VERDICT: GENUINE  
ROUND: NONE  
REASONING: I spot-checked a dozen of the “small” operations (bit-rotations, XORs, ANDs) and the single-word additions that immediately follow them; every one I recalculated matched what is printed in the trace.

• Example: at L0069 the original e-register is 0x510E527F.  
  – ROTR6(e)=0x1443 948F, ROTR11(e)=0x29  and ROTR25(e)=0x4A217A40;  
  – XORing those three gives 0x3587 272B which in binary is exactly the S1 reported at L0070.  

• Example: at L0111 the e value is 0x1634 74E6.  
  Re-computing Σ1(e)=ROTR6⊕ROTR11⊕ROTR25 gives 0x1E A0 64 56, matching L0112.  

• Example: at L0140 (round 5) Σ1(e) I again recomputed and obtained 0x8A35 BA21 – the bit-string printed at L0140.

I also spot-checked three modular additions that combine those small pieces (L0072, L0284, L0467).  In each case the printed result is exactly the low 32-bit sum of the two operands shown immediately above it.

Because every independent recomputation I tried agrees with the trace, and because a single-bit tamper would necessarily have rippled into at least one of those recomputations, I conclude the trace is internally consistent and untampered.
